msItem.title: Récits d’un ménestrel de Reims
msItem.note: Beginning missing. The text was identified by Johannes Junge Ruhland, and Lynn Butzlaff, ‘Un nouveau témoin des Récits d’un ménestrel de Reims : Oxford, Bodleian Library, MS Rawlinson B 170’, Zeitschrift für romanische Philologie, 142.1 (2026), pp. 1–41, doi:10.1515/zrp-2026-0001; they provide a detailed comparison with the printed edition of Natalis De Wailly, 'Notice sur six manuscrits contenant l’ouvrage anonyme publié par M. Louis Paris, sous le titre de « Chronique de Rains »', Notices et extraits des manuscrits de la Bibliothèque nationale 24 (1876), 289–340
msItem.explicit: ala a Roume et i demora grant piece

msItem.author: Brunetto Latini
msItem.title: Tresor
msItem.note: Extract: I.72-98, in the second redaction; ed. S. Baldwin and P. Barrette (2003), pp. 43-62 (see p. xxii on the two redactions). This manuscript not previously known.
msItem.incipit: Paul ses nons vaut autant a dire comme mervelles qui primierement avoit a non Saulus

msItem.title: Grandes Chroniques de France
msItem.note: Philip Augustus, fragmentary and disordered: (fols. 45r-64v) I.1-5, [text missing after fol. 50 due to the loss probably of four leaves], 9-20, III.1-2 (on fol. 63v, as shown by Ruhland and Butzlaff, the text skips mid-column from I.20 to III.1); [text lost]; (fols. 65r-72v) II.3-7; [text lost]; (fols. 73r-76v) III.2-4 (text following on from fol. 64v), ending imperfectly 'après les octaves de Paques. En cel consile fu traité'.

supportDesc.collation: [missing five (?) quires], 1(8)-6(8) (fols. 1-48), 7(8-4?) (fols. 49-52, probably missing third to sixth leaves), 8(8) (fols. 53-60), 9(four) (fols. 61-4), 10(8) (fols. 65-72), 11(four) (fols. 73-6). Quire number 'viii' (24v); catchwords (quires 1, 3, 4, 6, 7), leaf signatures (quires 5, 6, 8).

layoutDesc.layout: 2 cols. of 30-32 lines; ruled in leadpoint; ruled space c. 145 c. 115

handDesc.handNote: Northern textualis. Ruhland and Butzlaff distinguish three scribes (1) fols. 1r-29r; (2) fols. 29r-32v; (3) fols. 33r-44v, with fols. 45r-76v either by scribe 3 or a fourth scribe.

decoDesc.summary: Mutilated and damaged. (Pächt and Alexander i. 585) Originally 37 miniatures, all but three cut out: surviving miniatures on fols. 39v, 63r, 66v

history.provenance: In England in the late 17th or early 18th century: evidence of annotations (fols. 1r, 2r, 5v, 62v, etc.)

recordHist.source: Description by Matthew Holford, 2026. Previously described: W. D. Macray, Catalogi codicum manuscriptorum Bibliothecæ Bodleianæ...viri munificentissimi Ricardi Rawlinson, J.C.D., codicum...complectens, Quarto Catalogues V, 5 fascicles, 1862–1900). Summary Catalogue, Vol. 3, p. 196 Quarto Catalogue, Cols. 513–4

ref.title: JONAS: Répertoire des textes et des manuscrits médiévaux d'oc et d'oïl

revisionDesc.change: Toby Burrows/Mapping Manuscript MigrationsProvenance and acquisition information added using https://github.com/mapping-manuscript-migrations/oxford-tei-updates/blob/master/update_oxford_prov.rb in collaboration with the Mapping Manuscript Migrations project.
James Cummings Up-converted the markup using https://github.com/jamescummings/Bodleian-msDesc-ODD/blob/master/convertTolkien2Bodley.xsl
